titulo_real: Mauritius tells UK it will wait until end-July for Chagos deal
descripcion_real: Mauritius said it would wait up to the end of July for Britain to finalise a deal to hand over the Chagos Islands after London put the agreement on hold following objections from U.S. President Donald Trump.

titulo_real: EU leaders to discuss mutual assistance pact amid NATO doubts
descripcion_real: European Union leaders will discuss the bloc's mutual assistance clause at a summit in Cyprus on Thursday, as U.S. President Donald Trump's criticism of traditional allies raises concerns over his commitment to NATO at a time of growing insecurity.

titulo_real: Two Russian LGBT groups say courts declared them 'extremist'
descripcion_real: Two Russian LGBT organisations said on Thursday they had been designated by separate courts as "extremist", part of a widening crackdown on the gay and transgender community.

titulo_real: Prince Harry, on visit to Kyiv, tells Putin to 'stop this war'
descripcion_real: Britain's Prince Harry made an unannounced visit to Kyiv on Thursday and called on Russia's President Vladimir Putin to end the war - a week after a massive Russian aerial attack on the country - and on U.S. President Donald Trump to show leadership to help resolve the conflict.

titulo_real: Tanzania inquiry says over 500 killed in last year's election violence
descripcion_real: At least 518 people were killed in violence that broke out during Tanzania's elections last October, a government-appointed commission of inquiry said on Thursday, in the first official acknowledgment of the scale of the deadly unrest.

titulo_real: Lebanon to seek ceasefire extension in US-hosted talks with Israel
descripcion_real: The United States will host a second meeting between Lebanese and Israeli envoys on Thursday, with Beirut seeking the extension of a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, a day after Israeli strikes killed at least five people including a journalist.

titulo_real: Two trains collide in Denmark, injuring 18 people, emergency service says
descripcion_real: Two local trains collided head-on north of the Danish capital Copenhagen on Thursday, injuring 18 people, five of them critically, emergency services said.

titulo_real: Italy's contested deal to send migrants to Albania gets legal lifeline
descripcion_real: Italy's deal to send migrants to Albania while their asylum claims are processed is compatible in principle with EU rules, an adviser to the bloc’s top court said on Thursday, offering a lifeline to a policy repeatedly challenged in the courts.
